action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 8 completed 20 epochs of training with the improved MPNN (400 hidden, 6 layers, residual, mean+max pool). Validation AP peaked at 0.2079 (epoch 18) and slightly declined, suggesting the model is nearing its capacity limit. No eval has been submitted yet for this run. Analysis: I need the test-set avg_precision for this checkpoint to calibrate how much the 20-epoch run improved over the 10-epoch baseline (0.1767 AP). Since I have ~4.9 hours and 22 iterations remaining, spending one iteration on eval is safe and necessary before committing to longer training runs. I must NOT overwrite model.py now because predict.py needs to load the old checkpoint with the exact architecture that created it. Decision: Submit eval for checkpoints/best.pt from iteration 8. Keep all files unchanged. In the next iteration, I will implement a significantly stronger architecture (virtual node, GIN-style updates, wider/deeper) based on the test score received.
action.command: python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu'); print('Checkpoint OK. Best val AP:', ckpt.get('best_val_ap', 'N/A')); print('Epoch saved:', ckpt.get('epoch', 'N/A'))"

command_output.stdout:
Checkpoint OK. Best val AP: N/A
Epoch saved: N/A
